page.title: Interview Scheduling AI Agent
page.description: An intelligent scheduling platform for recruiting teams, covering recruiter operations, candidate self-scheduling, and exception recovery.

hero.title: Interview scheduling that feels instant to candidates and invisible to recruiters
hero.description: An AI recruiting workflow that reads stage rules, matches calendars, converts time zones, and handles reschedules without recruiter back-and-forth.

announcement.title: Recruiter console + candidate self-scheduling + exception handling

hero.tip: Includes realistic business scenarios, mock data, and Google sign-in so teams can experience the core workflow immediately.
hero.avatars_tip: Brings business rules, workflow logic, and product polish into one surface.

logos.title: Built to feel like a mature recruiting platform from the first screen

introduce.title: What this product is actually solving
introduce.label: Product Overview
introduce.description: Interview scheduling is a high-frequency recruiting workflow with clear rules, measurable friction, and real candidate-experience impact. This product turns that operational pain into a scalable scheduling capability.

items.title: Recruiter control tower
items.description: Show recommended time paths, fallback slots, and round-by-round status from a single scheduling console.

items.title: Candidate self-scheduling
items.description: Let candidates confirm compliant interview slots directly instead of waiting on email coordination.

items.title: Exception handling
items.description: Handle reschedules and no-shows with one automatic recovery path before escalating back to a recruiter.

items.title: Agent-visible reasoning
items.description: Expose the scoring logic, risk summary, and next actions so interviewers can understand why the plan is trustworthy.

benefits.title: Why this is a strong first agent workflow
benefits.label: Why This Works
benefits.description: The rules are structured enough for automation and the business value is immediate, making this a strong foundation for recruiting automation.

items.title: Clear operating rules
items.description: Availability matching, time zone conversion, stage order, and escalation policy are all explicit and defensible.

items.title: High-frequency admin work
items.description: Recruiters lose disproportionate time on scheduling coordination, even though it is not where their judgment is most valuable.

items.title: Candidate experience leverage
items.description: Faster, cleaner scheduling reduces drop-off risk and makes the overall hiring process feel more deliberate.

usage.title: The core path from intake to confirmed scheduling
usage.label: Platform Flow
usage.description: The homepage is structured like a real product so recruiting teams can understand setup, recommendation, confirmation, and recovery in one pass.

items.title: Load a realistic hiring scenario
items.description: Start from a mock candidate, role, timezone, SLA target, and market mix that looks like a real recruiting operation.

items.title: Review the recommended schedule path
items.description: See the deterministic round sequence, fallback options, and cross-timezone tradeoffs in the recruiter console.

items.title: Switch to the candidate flow
items.description: Validate that the candidate only sees a short list of compliant options instead of operational complexity.

items.title: Simulate exceptions
items.description: Demonstrate no-show recovery, reschedule handling, and recruiter escalation logic without needing live integrations.

features.title: Core capabilities already covered by the platform
features.description: Each capability maps to a real operational problem in recruiting rather than existing for show.

items.title: Stage-aware scheduling
items.description: Chain multiple interview rounds while respecting ordering and interviewer ownership.

items.title: Timezone-safe recommendations
items.description: Score options using candidate-friendly hours and explicit interviewer timezone visibility.

items.title: Fallback coverage
items.description: Keep alternative options visible so a recruiter can recover quickly when availability changes.

items.title: Candidate self-scheduling
items.description: Expose only a few compliant slots to the candidate instead of making them coordinate manually.

items.title: Planner brief generation
items.description: Generate recruiter-ready summaries, next actions, and risk notes from the current scheduling state.

items.title: Google sign-in surface
items.description: Supports a standard SaaS sign-in flow so teams can enter the platform through a familiar access layer.

stats.title: Signals that this is product thinking, not just UI polish
stats.description: Read the operational upside through workflow coverage, SLA pressure, and recovery depth.

items.description: Core surfaces: recruiter console, candidate scheduling, exception handling

items.title: 24h
items.description: Illustrative SLA target shown directly in the scheduling control tower
Automatic recovery attempt before the workflow escalates to a recruiter

testimonials.title: How different stakeholders read the value
testimonials.description: From recruiting operations to business leaders, the value is easy to see.

items.name: Amelia Chen
items.quote: The strongest part of this concept is that it removes coordination work without hiding the decision logic from recruiters.

items.name: Marcus Lee
items.quote: I can see the exact fallback path and SLA pressure at a glance, which is what makes the console feel operationally credible.

items.name: Sofia Martinez
items.quote: The candidate flow is intentionally simpler than the recruiter view, which is the right UX tradeoff for reducing friction.

items.name: David Wu
items.quote: This is a good agent wedge because ATS, calendar, and messaging integrations can be layered in after the operating model is proven.

items.name: Rachel Kim
items.quote: I like that the product does not claim to decide hiring outcomes. It focuses on reducing process drag, which is more believable.

items.name: Noah Thompson
items.quote: Within a few clicks I can see how the platform handles workflow logic, operational constraints, and recovery with discipline.

faq.title: Questions teams usually ask first
faq.description: These answers clarify where the platform fits, what it automates, and how it can expand.
faq.tip: Want more detail? Return to the platform overview above and walk through scheduling, candidate selection, and no-show recovery.

items.question: Why choose interview scheduling as the first agent use case?
items.answer: Because it has explicit rules, high workflow frequency, and visible business impact. That makes it easier to launch safely while still delivering real value.

items.question: What is actually automated versus still rule-based?
items.answer: Slot selection is deterministic and rule-driven, while the AI layer focuses on summaries, messaging, and risk framing. That boundary keeps the experience trustworthy.

items.question: How does the candidate experience stay simple?
items.answer: The system hides operational complexity and only presents a small set of valid options. Candidates get speed and clarity, while recruiters keep control of the constraints.

items.question: How does exception handling work in this MVP?
items.answer: The agent can reschedule once automatically on a no-show or conflict using the next-best compliant slot. Complex cases are then escalated back to a recruiter.

items.question: Is this already integrated with ATS and calendars?
items.answer: The current release uses high-quality simulated data to show the core operating model. ATS, calendar, and messaging integrations can be layered in as the system expands.

items.question: Why support Google sign-in if the core workflow is open?
items.answer: The current release prioritizes fast access to the core workflow. Sign-in lays the groundwork for team workspaces, permissions, and activity history.

cta.title: Walk the product in under five minutes
cta.description: Enter the platform, switch across hiring scenarios, and review automated scheduling, candidate selection, and exception recovery in one flow.
